source.title: Structural Damage - Crane/Lifting Casing Jacks Incident at Platform Gail

context.region: Pacific OCS Region, Gulf of Mexico

event.top_event: Amputation injury during conductor removal operations
actions_taken: Immediate work stand down on conductor removal operations
Evacuation by boat to M/V Glenn C
Recovery of amputated digits for attempted reattachment
Resumption of operations using chain-link fence fabric method on July 7, 2023
event.summary: On June 18, 2023 at 18:35 hours, an injured person (IP) suffered amputation of the middle and ring finger of his left hand while adjusting chains on a marine growth removal system (MGRS) during conductor removal operations at Platform Gail. The IP placed his left hand fingers under the chain during an assumed pause in conductor lifting. The conductor resumed rising, tightening the chain and trapping the IP's hand, resulting in amputation of finger tips between the fingernail and first knuckle. The incident occurred due to inadequate verbal communication regarding the 'all stop' command, lack of risk assessment for the MGRS methods employed, and deviation from the approved Application for Permit to Modify (APM) which specified use of diver methods with water jets.
recommendations: Develop and implement comprehensive written management of change (MOC) procedures for switching between MGRS methods
Conduct formal risk and hazards analysis on all MGRS methods prior to use, in accordance with API RP 75
Improve radio communication protocols with explicit confirmation of 'all stop' commands by the Weatherford Lead
Implement enhanced guarding or engineering controls to prevent worker proximity to moving machinery and tensioned chains
Ensure compliance with approved APM and notify BSEE of any deviations or alternative methods
Provide additional training on communication procedures and hazard recognition for all personnel involved in conductor removal

hazards.name: Moving conductor and associated lifting equipment
Marine growth removal system with moving parts
Inadequate work environment lighting and wet conditions

threats.name: Failure to confirm 'all stop' command over radio
Worker assumption that conductor movement had stopped
Inadequate risk assessment of MGRS methods
Lack of management of change procedures for MGRS method switching
Deviation from approved APM without notification to BSEE
Worker proximity to moving conductor and tensioned chains

consequences.name: Amputation of two fingers (middle and ring finger)
Permanent disability and loss of manual dexterity
Work stand down and operational delay
Regulatory non-compliance and potential enforcement action

controls.name: Pre-shift safety briefing and pass-over information
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure personnel awareness of collar location and need for 'all stop'

supporting_text: At 18:00, the night shift conductor removal crew held a safety meeting and received pass-over information from the day shift. During the meeting, personnel discussed that a collar was just below the +44 platform level, and all involved will need to be ready for an 'all stop' to make adjustments.

controls.name: Radio communication protocol for 'all stop' commands
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to halt conductor movement when adjustments needed
controls.lod_basis: Administrative procedure requiring confirmation of 'all stop' by Weatherford Lead

supporting_text: A call for an 'all stop' came over the radio from the trainee on the +44 level, but no one confirmed it.
According to Chevron's procedures, any station can request an 'all stop,' and the Weatherford Lead will acknowledge the 'all stop' and select a control to stop the casing jack.
The Weatherford Lead reported in his witness statement that the only 'all stop' he heard was from the trainee who had reported that there had been an injury.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for marine growth removal task
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify and communicate hazards to workers

supporting_text: BSEE reviewed the JSAs and work permits for the task of marine growth removal and determined that the JSAs cautioned workers, including IP, about hand placement and good communication prior to the incident.

BSEE's reliance on the JSAs and work permits for conductor removal operations was insufficient to identify and evaluate risks and ensure that safeguards were in place for the specific activity of marine growth removal.

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) procedures for MGRS method selection
controls.barrier_role: Risk assessment and approval of operational changes
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory requirement per 30 CFR § 250.1912 and API RP 75

supporting_text: Chevron did not produce any MOC records, stating that it did not perform a comprehensive risk analysis of changing from one MGRS method to another.
Chevron switched through three different MGRS methods without developing and implementing written MOC procedures, as required by 30 CFR § 250.1912(a)(2).

controls.name: Approved Application for Permit to Modify (APM) compliance
controls.barrier_role: Regulatory oversight and notification of operational changes
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory requirement to notify BSEE/BOEM of deviations from approved APM

supporting_text: At no time prior to or during conductor removal operations did Chevron submit to BOEM or BSEE an amended APM with documentation stating that it was going to use, or was considering using, MGRS methods other than the diver method.
Chevron deviated from its approved APM and failed to provide any notification to BSEE of the amended course of action in which it intended to engage in cleaning the conductors.

controls.name: Trainee supervision and experienced worker presence
controls.lod_basis: Human supervision to ensure safe work practices

supporting_text: The CEC contractor was a trainee working on the MGRS for the first time and was waiting for the experienced worker (IP) to show up.
At approximately 18:30, IP arrived on station and told the trainee to call out over the radio to the Weatherford Lead that the +44 level was 'all clear.'

controls.name: Chain-link fence fabric MGRS method (hands-off approach)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to reduce worker proximity to moving machinery
controls.lod_basis: Engineering design to minimize worker exposure to hazards

supporting_text: This MGRS allowed CEC contractors to not have to work near the conductor's movement.
When conductor removal operations recommenced after the safety stand down on July 7, 2023, following the incident, Chevron resumed using the chain-link fence fabric method.

controls.name: Medical response and evacuation procedures
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: Operations were stopped and IP was helped to make his way to the medic. After administering first aid and a pain killer, the medic decided to evacuate IP by boat.
At 19:46, IP was transferred by crane on to the M/V Glenn C and escorted to shore with the medic and IP's supervisor. An ambulance waiting onshore then transported IP to the Ventura hospital.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
